name: Powell Valley National Bank
description_short: Powell Valley National Bank is a community bank established in 1888 serving Southwest Virginia, East Tennessee, and expanding into Virginia's Tidewater region with personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Powell Valley National Bank was founded in 1888 in Jonesville, Virginia, making it the oldest bank in far Southwest Virginia, the 4th oldest in Virginia, and the 5th oldest operating in Tennessee. The bank has survived 31 economic recessions and maintains one of the strongest capital ratios in the United States. With headquarters in Jonesville, the bank has expanded significantly, operating full-service branches in Pennington Gap, Wise, Big Stone Gap, Duffield, Kingsport (TN), and Abingdon (VA), along with loan production offices in Bristol (TN) and Chesapeake (VA) as of 2025.

Powell Valley National Bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, instant issue debit cards, credit cards, mortgage loans, and personal loans. On the business side, they provide business checking accounts, business credit cards, commercial lending, agriculture loans, and merchant services. The bank also offers digital convenience features such as online banking, mobile banking, online bill pay, Zelle transfers, and mobile deposit for business customers. In 2014, the bank created Powell Valley Investment Services through its parent company Powell Valley Bankshares, Inc., offering securities through Osaic Institutions, Inc.

Powell Valley National Bank distinguishes itself through its deep historical roots and regional dominance. According to FDIC deposit reports, it holds the #1 market deposit share in Lee, Scott, and Wise counties. The bank's longevity through multiple economic cycles and its strong capital position reflect stability and conservative management. Their expansion strategy in recent years—opening offices in Kingsport (2018), Abingdon (2019), Bristol (2024), and Chesapeake (2025)—demonstrates active growth beyond their traditional Southwest Virginia base. The bank emphasizes community banking values while offering modern digital banking conveniences.

Powell Valley National Bank is a legitimate, FDIC-insured community bank suitable for consumers seeking stable, relationship-based banking in its service regions. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank primarily serves Southwest Virginia and East Tennessee, with recent expansion into other Virginia markets. Consumers outside these regions will have limited access to physical branches, though online and mobile banking options may mitigate this. The bank's community-focused model may mean fewer perks or promotional rates compared to large national banks, though stability and personalized service may offset this for regional customers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with instant issue debit cards
Savings accounts and personal investment services
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance
Personal loans for consolidation and other purposes
Business checking accounts and business credit cards
Online banking and online bill pay with Zelle transfers
Mobile banking and PVB Mobile Banking app
Securities and investment services through Powell Valley Investment Services
pros: Established in 1888 with 137+ years of community banking experience and proven stability through 31 economic recessions
One of the strongest capital ratios in the United States, indicating financial strength and safety
#1 market deposit share in Lee, Scott, and Wise counties, demonstrating regional trust and dominance
Expanding branch network with 2024-2025 openings in Bristol (TN) and Chesapeake (VA) showing growth trajectory
Comprehensive digital banking suite including online banking, mobile banking, Zelle, online bill pay, and card controls
Full-service offerings for both personal and business customers, including agriculture lending specialization
Securities and investment services available through Powell Valley Investment Services affiliate (Osaic)
cons: Limited geographic footprint primarily concentrated in Southwest Virginia and East Tennessee, restricting accessibility for out-of-region customers
No mention of competitive rate information, promotional offers, or how their rates compare to national banks
Relatively small regional bank with fewer resources and product innovation compared to large national banking institutions
Active fraud alert on website regarding phone spoofing indicates recent security threats targeting customers
Limited information about specific fees, minimum balances, or account requirements on public website
best_for: Southwest Virginia and East Tennessee residents seeking stable, relationship-based community banking with local decision-making
Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized lending and business banking services in rural regions
Small to mid-size business owners preferring personalized service and local banking relationships over national banks
Conservative investors valuing bank stability and capital strength over aggressive rate competition or promotional offers
diagnosis: Powell Valley National Bank is best for consumers and businesses in Southwest Virginia, East Tennessee, and expanding Virginia markets seeking stable, relationship-focused community banking backed by 137+ years of history and strong capital reserves. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside their service regions will find limited branch access, and the bank's smaller size means fewer promotional rates and products compared to national competitors, though stability and personalized service are compensating advantages for regional users.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
